do: Keep body text at 4.5:1 or higher against its background
dont: Place light gray text on white

do: Show a clear focus ring on every interactive element
dont: Strip the focus outline for looks

do: Describe the meaning of informative images
dont: Leave alt empty on meaningful images

do: Wrap regions in header main nav and footer
dont: Build the whole page from div soup

do: Associate a visible label with every field
dont: Rely on placeholder text as the label

do: Honor the reduced motion preference
dont: Force large animations on everyone

do: Use relative units so text scales with zoom
dont: Lock font sizes in fixed pixels

do: Prefer native elements before adding ARIA
dont: Reinvent a button from a div and role

do: Validate on blur and explain how to fix it
dont: Reveal all errors only after submit

do: State the problem and the fix in plain words
dont: Show a generic invalid input message

do: Mark required fields and keep forms short
dont: Make every field required by default

do: Set input type so the right keyboard appears
dont: Use plain text for email and phone

do: Add autocomplete hints for known fields
dont: Block autofill on common fields

do: Disable submit while the request is pending
dont: Let users double submit a form

do: Offer a show-password toggle
dont: Hide the password with no way to check

do: Mark the current page in the navigation
dont: Leave users guessing where they are

do: Show breadcrumbs for deep hierarchies
dont: Drop users deep with no path back

do: Preserve scroll and state on back
dont: Reset the page on every back press

do: Provide a skip-to-content link first
dont: Force tabbing through every menu item

do: Keep navigation to two or three levels
dont: Bury items four menus deep

do: Confirm that an action actually succeeded
dont: Leave the user unsure it worked

do: Offer a clear retry or next step on error
dont: Show a dead-end error with no action

do: Reflect the change instantly then reconcile
dont: Freeze the UI until the server replies

do: Let important messages stay until dismissed
dont: Auto-hide critical messages too fast

do: Confirm or offer undo for destructive acts
dont: Delete permanently on one tap

do: Keep UI transitions short and purposeful
dont: Make every transition slow and showy

do: Use ease-out for entering elements
dont: Use linear easing for UI motion

do: Show progress for waits beyond a second
dont: Spin forever with no context

do: Use scroll effects sparingly and subtly
dont: Stack heavy parallax on every section

do: Keep body lines around 60 to 75 characters
dont: Run text the full width of wide screens

do: Set body line height near 1.5
dont: Pack lines tightly with no leading

do: Keep body text at least 16px
dont: Shrink body copy below readable size

do: Use a consistent modular type scale
dont: Pick arbitrary sizes per element

do: Use font-display swap with a fallback
dont: Block render until web fonts load

do: Pair color with text or icons for meaning
dont: Use color alone to convey state

do: Test contrast in both light and dark themes
dont: Invert colors and ship it untested

do: Constrain the palette to defined tokens
dont: Introduce one-off hex values ad hoc

do: Reserve red and green for clear semantics
dont: Use semantic colors decoratively

do: Give content room with consistent spacing
dont: Cram elements edge to edge

do: Use a spacing scale like 4 or 8px steps
dont: Use random pixel gaps everywhere

do: Align elements to a shared grid
dont: Let elements drift off any grid

do: Design fluid layouts that adapt smoothly
dont: Build only for one fixed width

do: Make the primary action the most prominent
dont: Give every button equal weight

do: Make tap targets at least 44 by 44px
dont: Place tiny taps close together

do: Keep primary actions in the thumb zone
dont: Put key actions in the top corners

do: Reveal hidden gestures with visible cues
dont: Hide core actions behind secret swipes

do: Allow pinch zoom on mobile pages
dont: Disable user scaling in the viewport

do: Guide the next step in empty states
dont: Show a blank screen with no content

do: Show skeletons that match the final layout
dont: Flash a blank then a layout shift

do: Explain failures and offer recovery
dont: Show a raw stack trace to users

do: Write button labels that name the action
dont: Use vague labels like submit or ok

do: Keep one consistent voice across the app
dont: Mix formal and casual at random

do: Lazy-load and serve modern image formats
dont: Ship huge unoptimized hero images

do: Reserve space for media to avoid shift
dont: Let late content jump the layout

do: Do not hide essential actions behind hover
dont: Require hover to reveal core actions

do: Extend the clickable area to the whole control
dont: Make only the tiny icon clickable

do: Reveal advanced options on demand
dont: Dump every option on one screen

do: Use spacing and alignment to aid scanning
dont: Pack dense tables with no breathing room
